examples.title: Algorithm: Storm Topology Processing Flow
examples.description: Demonstrates how data flows through a Storm topology from spouts to bolts with tuple processing
examples.code:
ALGORITHM StormTopologyProcessing
INPUT: Raw sensor data stream
OUTPUT: Processed, aggregated results

BEGIN
    // === TOPOLOGY DEFINITION ===
    
    // Step 1: Create Topology Builder
    topologyBuilder = new TopologyBuilder()
    
    // Step 2: Add Spout (Data Source)
    spoutId = "sensor-spout"
    parallelism = 3
    topologyBuilder.setSpout(spoutId, SensorSpout(), parallelism)
    
    // Step 3: Add First Bolt (Processing)
    bolt1Id = "filter-bolt"
    parallelism = 4
    topologyBuilder.setBolt(bolt1Id, FilterBolt(), parallelism)
        .shuffleGrouping(spoutId)
    
    // Step 4: Add Second Bolt (Aggregation with Fields Grouping)
    bolt2Id = "aggregate-bolt"
    parallelism = 2
    topologyBuilder.setBolt(bolt2Id, AggregateBolt(), parallelism)
        .fieldsGrouping(bolt1Id, ["sensor_id"])
    
    // Step 5: Add Final Bolt (Output)
    bolt3Id = "output-bolt"
    parallelism = 1
    topologyBuilder.setBolt(bolt3Id, OutputBolt(), parallelism)
        .globalGrouping(bolt2Id)
    
    // === RUNTIME EXECUTION ===
    
    // Submit topology to cluster
    StormSubmitter.submitTopology("iot-topology", config, topologyBuilder.createTopology())
    
END

ALGORITHM TupleProcessingWithAckFail
INPUT: Input tuple from upstream
OUTPUT: Processed tuple or failure indication

BEGIN
    // Receive tuple
    tuple = receiveTuple()
    tupleId = tuple.getMessageId()
    
    TRY
        // Process the tuple
        result = processData(tuple.values)
        
        // Emit new tuple anchored to input tuple
        emit(result, anchor=tuple)
        
        // Acknowledge successful processing
        ack(tupleId)
        
    EXCEPTION
        // Processing failed
        fail(tupleId)
        // Tuple will be replayed by upstream spout
    
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows two key algorithms: First, how to define a Storm topology with spouts and bolts connected through stream groupings (shuffle, fields, and global). Second, the ack/fail mechanism that guarantees message processing - when a bolt successfully processes a tuple it sends an ack, otherwise it sends a fail which triggers replay from the spout.

examples.title: Python: IoT Sensor Data Processing Topology
examples.description: Implements a complete Storm topology for processing IoT sensor data with filtering and aggregation
examples.code:
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime
import time
import random

# Simulated Storm Spout - reads from external IoT sensor source
class SensorSpout:
    """Spout that emits sensor data tuples"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.sensors = [f"sensor_{i}" for i in range(100)]
        self.emitted_tuples = {}  # Track for replay
        
    def next_tuple(self):
        """Emit sensor reading as a tuple"""
        sensor_id = random.choice(self.sensors)
        temperature = round(random.uniform(-10, 60), 2)
        humidity = round(random.uniform(0, 100), 2)
        timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
        
        # Create tuple with unique message ID for tracking
        tuple_id = f"{sensor_id}_{timestamp}"
        values = [sensor_id, temperature, humidity, timestamp]
        
        # Track for potential replay
        self.emitted_tuples[tuple_id] = values
        
        return tuple_id, values
    
    def ack(self, tuple_id):
        """Called when tuple processing succeeded"""
        if tuple_id in self.emitted_tuples:
            del self.emitted_tuples[tuple_id]
            print(f"[ACK] Tuple {tuple_id} processed successfully")
    
    def fail(self, tuple_id):
        """Called when tuple processing failed - trigger replay"""
        print(f"[FAIL] Tuple {tuple_id} failed, will replay")
        # In real Storm, tuple would be replayed from here


# Simulated Bolt - filters异常 temperature readings
class TemperatureFilterBolt:
    """Filters and processes temperature data"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.anomaly_threshold_high = 50.0
        self.anomaly_threshold_low = -5.0
        self.processed_count = 0
        
    def process(self, tuple_id, values):
        """Process incoming sensor tuple"""
        sensor_id, temperature, humidity, timestamp = values
        self.processed_count += 1
        
        # Check for anomalies
        if temperature > self.anomaly_threshold_high:
            status = "HIGH_TEMP_ALERT"
            self.emit("anomaly-stream", [sensor_id, temperature, status])
        elif temperature < self.anomaly_threshold_low:
            status = "LOW_TEMP_ALERT"
            self.emit("anomaly-stream", [sensor_id, temperature, status])
        else:
            status = "NORMAL"
            self.emit("normal-stream", [sensor_id, temperature, humidity])
        
        # Acknowledge processing
        return True  # Signal ack to spout


# Simulated Bolt - aggregates data by sensor
class AggregationBolt:
    """Aggregates sensor readings by sensor_id (Fields Grouping)"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        # Simulates fields grouping: same sensor_id always goes to same task
        self.sensor_data = defaultdict(list)
        self.window_size = 10  # Aggregate every 10 readings
        
    def process(self, tuple_id, values):
        """Aggregate incoming data by sensor ID"""
        sensor_id = values[0]
        temperature = values[1]
        
        # Store reading (in real Storm, this would be time-windowed)
        self.sensor_data[sensor_id].append(temperature)
        
        # Emit aggregated result when window is full
        if len(self.sensor_data[sensor_id]) >= self.window_size:
            readings = self.sensor_data[sensor_id]
            avg_temp = sum(readings) / len(readings)
            max_temp = max(readings)
            min_temp = min(readings)
            
            self.emit("aggregated-stream", [
                sensor_id,
                round(avg_temp, 2),
                round(max_temp, 2),
                round(min_temp, 2),
                len(readings)
            ])
            
            # Clear buffer
            self.sensor_data[sensor_id] = []
        
        return True


# Simulated Stream Grouping
class StreamGroupings:
    """Demonstrates different stream grouping strategies"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def shuffle_grouping(tasks, tuple_data):
        """Random distribution across tasks (load balancing)"""
        target_task = random.choice(tasks)
        return target_task
    
    @staticmethod
    def fields_grouping(tasks, values, fields):
        """Group by field value - ensures same key goes to same task"""
        # Hash the field value to determine task
        field_value = values[0]  # sensor_id
        task_index = hash(field_value) % len(tasks)
        return tasks[task_index]
    
    @staticmethod
    def global_grouping(tasks):
        """Send all tuples to single task (lowest ID)"""
        return min(tasks)
    
    @staticmethod
    def all_grouping(tasks):
        """Broadcast to all tasks"""
        return tasks  # All tasks receive the tuple


# Example execution
def run_storm_topology():
    """Simulate running the Storm topology"""
    print("=== Starting IoT Storm Topology ===\n")
    
    # Initialize components
    spout = SensorSpout()
    filter_bolt = TemperatureFilterBolt()
    agg_bolt = AggregationBolt()
    
    # Simulate processing 20 tuples
    for i in range(20):
        # Spout emits tuple
        tuple_id, values = spout.next_tuple()
        print(f"[SPOUT] Emitted: {values}")
        
        # Filter bolt processes (simulating shuffle grouping)
        success = filter_bolt.process(tuple_id, values)
        if success:
            spout.ack(tuple_id)
        else:
            spout.fail(tuple_id)
        
        time.sleep(0.1)  # Simulate processing time
    
    print("\n=== Topology Execution Complete ===")
    print(f"Filter bolt processed: {filter_bolt.processed_count} tuples")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_storm_topology()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the core Storm concepts: Spouts as data sources that emit tuples, Bolts as processing units that transform data, and Stream Groupings (shuffle, fields, global, all) that determine tuple distribution. It shows the ack/fail mechanism for guaranteed processing and simulates how fields grouping ensures same sensor_id routes to same task for proper aggregation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per tuple for shuffle/global, O(n) for fields grouping
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is window size for aggregation bolts

examples.title: Java: Storm Topology with Custom Spout and Bolts
examples.description: Implements a complete Java Storm topology for real-time log processing with all stream groupings
examples.explanation: This Java implementation shows how to build a real Storm topology using the Storm API. Key features: 1) LogSpout extends BaseRichSpout to emit tuples from external sources with message IDs for tracking. 2) LogFilterBolt demonstrates filtering and multi-stream output (critical vs normal). 3) ErrorCounterBolt shows fields grouping - all logs for same service route to same task for accurate counting. 4) LoggingBolt uses global grouping to collect all results in one place. The topology uses shuffle, fields, and global groupings to demonstrate different routing strategies.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for tuple processing in each bolt
examples.spaceComplexity: O(s) where s is number of unique services for counting
